div1.entryFree: Crow, vb. (Impf. crew: Hml. I, 1, 147. I, 2, 218. Partic. crowed: Rom. IV, 4, 3) to cry as a cock: Tp. II, 1, 29. Mids. II, 1, 267. Shr. II, 228. H5 IV Chor. H5 IV Chor. Rom. IV, 4, 3. Hml. I, 1, 147. Hml. I, 1, 147 I, 2, 218. Cymb. II, 1, 26. == to laugh merrily: you were wont, when you laughed, to c. like a cock, Gentl. II, 1, 28. my lungs began to c. like chanticleer, As II, 7, 30. these wise men that c. so at these set kind of fools, Tw. I, 5, 95. == to strut like a cock crowing in triumph: and yet he'll be --ing as if he had writ man ever since his father was a bachelor, H4B I, 2, 30.
